title: Validate dependency references at bead creation and scheduler startup
description:
The scheduler crashes with ValueError when a bead references a dependency that does not exist in storage (e.g. 'Bead not found: B-6ad73fa1-docs'). This happens because the planner can generate review beads that reference per-developer doc beads as dependencies, but those doc beads are never created (suppressed by planner-owned followup logic). The missing reference is only discovered at runtime when the scheduler iterates ready beads.

Two fixes needed:

1. **Scheduler startup validation**: In RepositoryStorage.ready_beads() or Scheduler.run_once(), catch ValueError from load_bead() when checking dependencies. Instead of crashing, log a warning event to execution_history on the affected bead (e.g. 'dependency_missing: B-xxx not found') and skip that bead for the current cycle rather than raising. This keeps the scheduler running even with broken references.

2. **Write-time validation**: In RepositoryStorage.create_bead() and RepositoryStorage.save_bead(), validate that all bead IDs listed in dependencies actually exist in storage. If any are missing, raise a ValueError listing the missing IDs before persisting. This catches the problem at the source (planner write time) rather than at runtime.

The write-time check is the primary fix. The runtime resilience is defence-in-depth so a single corrupt bead cannot crash the entire scheduler.
acceptance_criteria: RepositoryStorage.create_bead() raises ValueError listing missing dependency IDs when any dependency does not exist
Scheduler does not crash with ValueError when a bead has a missing dependency reference — it skips the bead and continues
A warning event is recorded in the affected bead's execution_history when a dependency is missing at runtime
New tests cover both the write-time validation and the runtime skip behaviour

handoff_summary.completed: Added write-time dependency checks in RepositoryStorage.save_bead()/create_bead() that raise ValueError listing missing dependency bead IDs before persistence. Added runtime handling in RepositoryStorage.dependency_satisfied()/ready_beads() to record a single dependency_missing warning event on corrupt ready beads and skip them for the current cycle. Added regression coverage for create/save validation and scheduler skip behavior by seeding a corrupt bead file directly.
handoff_summary.remaining: Tester should run the targeted regression suite covering dependency validation and scheduler runtime skip behavior, plus any broader suite required by the pipeline.
handoff_summary.risks: save_bead now enforces dependency existence for every persistence path, so any legacy or manually edited bead JSON with broken dependencies will fail normal save/update flows until repaired; the runtime warning path only preserves scheduler startup by writing the warning through the internal raw persistence helper.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run the targeted tests for dependency validation and corrupt-bead scheduler resilience, then review whether any legacy persisted beads need cleanup tooling.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: RepositoryStorage.save_bead() is now stricter and will reject missing dependency IDs across all callers. Downstream agents touching bead persistence should expect ValueError on invalid dependency lists. Scheduler.py was not changed; the runtime defense lives in storage.ready_beads().

conflict_risks: RepositoryStorage.save_bead() is now stricter and will reject missing dependency IDs across all callers. Downstream agents touching bead persistence should expect ValueError on invalid dependency lists. Scheduler.py was not changed; the runtime defense lives in storage.ready_beads().

execution_history.summary: Lease expired and bead was requeued
Implemented dependency reference validation in storage writes and added runtime resilience so corrupt beads with missing dependencies are warned and skipped instead of crashing scheduler selection.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
